variationDiversity.description: Are variations MEANINGFULLY different in layout, hierarchy, and IA — not just cosmetic shuffles?

scoring.10: Each variation explores a fundamentally different approach to the same problem
scoring.7: Most variations differ meaningfully, 1 feels like a minor tweak
scoring.4: Variations differ in arrangement but not in approach
scoring.1: All variations are essentially the same layout with different spacing/colors

scamperApplication.description: Does each variation apply a specific, named SCAMPER lens with clear rationale?

scoring.10: Each variation names its SCAMPER lens and explains what specifically was substituted/eliminated/etc.
scoring.7: SCAMPER lenses named but rationale is thin
scoring.4: Lenses mentioned but don't actually drive the variation

jtbdAlignment.description: Does each variation state which user job (Monitor/Investigate/Act/Configure/Learn/Decide) it optimizes for?

scoring.10: Each variation explicitly names its target job and explains how the design serves it
scoring.7: Jobs mentioned but connection to design decisions is implicit
scoring.4: Generic job assignment without design reasoning

contradictionHandling.description: When constraints conflict, does the skill identify and address the tension?

scoring.10: Explicitly names the contradiction, explores creative resolutions, asks user to prioritize
scoring.7: Acknowledges tension but doesn't fully resolve it
scoring.0: N/A — no contradictions in the test case

recommendationQuality.description: Is the recommendation grounded in user behavior, not aesthetic preference?

scoring.10: 'Variation 2 works for ops teams because the status bar eliminates scrolling before knowing if something's broken'
scoring.7: Recommendation cites user behavior but could be more specific

buildability.description: Is each variation a complete, buildable design — not a half-thought?

scoring.10: Each variation has specific components, tokens, text content, and could be built immediately
scoring.1: Abstract ideas without concrete design decisions
